T3 Experiment Pilot: Condition Comparison

Contact vs. Chat vs. Game — Pre-Post Changes by Condition

Published

February 19, 2026

Data: | Conditions:

Sample

Code 
n_total <- nrow(dat)
n_by_condition <- dat %>% count(treatment)
n_contact <- n_by_condition %>% filter(treatment == "Contact") %>% pull(n)
if (!pool_controls) {
  n_chat <- n_by_condition %>% filter(treatment == "Chat") %>% pull(n)
  n_game <- n_by_condition %>% filter(treatment == "Game") %>% pull(n)
} else {
  n_control <- n_by_condition %>% filter(treatment == "Control") %>% pull(n)
}
mean_age   <- mean(dat$age, na.rm = TRUE)
pct_female <- mean(dat$gender == "Female", na.rm = TRUE) * 100

We recruited 401 participants from Prolific: 208 in the Contact condition and 193 in the Control condition (Chat + Game pooled; mean age = 42.20, 52% female).

Code 
dat %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    `Democrats (D→R)` = sum(learner_party == "D→R"),
    `Republicans (R→D)` = sum(learner_party == "R→D"),
    `Mean Age` = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE) %>% numformat(),
    `% Female` = (sum(gender == "Female", na.rm = TRUE) / n() * 100) %>% numformat(0),
    `Extremism (M)` = mean(political_extremism, na.rm = TRUE) %>% numformat(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Sample Composition by Condition")
Sample Composition by Condition
treatment N Democrats (D→R) Republicans (R→D) Mean Age % Female Extremism (M)
Contact 208 99 109 41.30 50 76.36
Control 193 103 90 43.17 53 77.84
Code 
# Actual green score by party (from own responses)
actual_by_party <- dat %>%
  group_by(learner_party) %>%
  summarise(
    actual = mean(actual_green, na.rm = TRUE) %>% numformat(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

dat %>%
  group_by(treatment, learner_party) %>%
  summarise(
    `Baseline belief (M)` = mean(green_outgroup_pre, na.rm = TRUE) %>% numformat(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(actual_by_party, by = "learner_party") %>%
  rename(Condition = treatment, `Learner party` = learner_party,
         `Actual outgroup mean` = actual) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Baseline Outgroup Env. Beliefs by Condition and Party",
             subtitle = "Composite mean of env1–6 (1–5 scale); higher = more pro-environment attributed to outgroup") %>%
  tab_footnote("Actual = true mean from the outgroup's own responses. D→R = Democrats estimating Republicans; R→D = Republicans estimating Democrats.",
               locations = cells_title())
Mean baseline outgroup environmental attitude beliefs (composite of env1–6, 1–5 scale) by condition and learner party. ‘Actual’ is the true mean of the outgroup’s own responses (Republicans’ actual for D→R; Democrats’ actual for R→D).
Baseline Outgroup Env. Beliefs by Condition and Party1
Composite mean of env1–6 (1–5 scale); higher = more pro-environment attributed to outgroup1
Condition Learner party Baseline belief (M) Actual outgroup mean
Contact D→R 2.10 3.59
Contact R→D 3.86 4.06
Control D→R 2.09 3.59
Control R→D 3.94 4.06
1 Actual = true mean from the outgroup's own responses. D→R = Democrats estimating Republicans; R→D = Republicans estimating Democrats.

Condition balance check

Code 
# Check randomization: are conditions balanced on baseline measures?
bal_acc  <- aov(accuracy_pre ~ treatment, data = dat)
bal_warm <- aov(warmth_outgroup_pre ~ treatment, data = dat)
bal_ext  <- aov(political_extremism ~ treatment, data = dat)

bal_acc_p  <- summary(bal_acc)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
bal_warm_p <- summary(bal_warm)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
bal_ext_p  <- summary(bal_ext)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]

Conditions did not differ significantly on baseline accuracy (F = 0.672), baseline warmth (F = 0.020), or political extremism (F = 0.537), supporting successful randomization.

Differential engagement / cheating check

Code 
# Survey phase: tab switches and hidden time (same for all conditions)
eng_survey_aov_switch <- aov(tab_switch_count   ~ treatment, data = dat)
eng_survey_aov_hidden <- aov(I(time_hidden_ms / 1000) ~ treatment, data = dat)
eng_survey_switch_p <- summary(eng_survey_aov_switch)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
eng_survey_hidden_p <- summary(eng_survey_aov_hidden)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]

# Intervention phase: tab switches and time in intervention window
eng_bot_aov_switch <- aov(tab_switch_count_bot ~ treatment, data = dat)
eng_bot_aov_hidden <- aov(I(time_hidden_ms_bot / 60000) ~ treatment, data = dat)
eng_bot_switch_p <- summary(eng_bot_aov_switch)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
eng_bot_hidden_p <- summary(eng_bot_aov_hidden)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]

# Pairwise for bot tab switches (the only marginally significant survey metric)
eng_bot_switch_tukey <- dat %>% tukey_hsd(tab_switch_count_bot ~ treatment)
if (!pool_controls) {
  eng_bot_contact_game_p <- eng_bot_switch_tukey %>%
    filter(group1 == "Contact", group2 == "Game") %>% pull(p.adj)
} else {
  eng_bot_contact_game_p <- eng_bot_switch_tukey %>%
    filter(group1 == "Contact", group2 == "Control") %>% pull(p.adj)
}

# Summary table
eng_summary <- dat %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    `Survey switches (M)` = mean(tab_switch_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Survey hidden (sec, M)` = mean(time_hidden_ms / 1000, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Intervention switches (M)` = mean(tab_switch_count_bot, na.rm = TRUE),
    `Intervention time in window (min, M)` = mean(time_hidden_ms_bot / 60000, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
Code 
eng_summary %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.double), ~ numformat(.x))) %>%
  rename(Condition = treatment) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Engagement by Condition") %>%
  tab_footnote(
    "Survey phase: tab switches and hidden time during the questionnaire. Intervention phase: switches and time during the treatment window.",
    locations = cells_title()
  )
Engagement by Condition1
Condition Survey switches (M) Survey hidden (sec, M) Intervention switches (M) Intervention time in window (min, M)
Contact 0.21 2.85 1.45 4.49
Control 0.12 1.86 0.70 0.28
1 Survey phase: tab switches and hidden time during the questionnaire. Intervention phase: switches and time during the treatment window.

Survey phase (left the survey screen): conditions did not differ in tab switches (F = 0.204) or hidden time (F = 0.382), ruling out differential cheating during measurement.

Intervention phase: time spent in the intervention window differed across conditions (F < .001), but all conditions were fixed at 10 minutes, so differences in the hidden-time metric reflect how participants engaged within the window (e.g., Contact participants may have kept the chat window active more continuously), not differential exposure time. Intervention tab switches were marginally higher in Contact vs. Control (p = 0.004, Tukey-corrected), consistent with the chatbot task requiring active back-and-forth engagement. No evidence of differential cheating by condition.

Outlier removal

Code 
dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    acc_change  = accuracy_post - accuracy_pre,
    warm_change = warmth_outgroup_post - warmth_outgroup_pre
  )

# ── Cook's D > 4/n from ANCOVA (accuracy and warmth) ─────────────────────────
ancova_acc  <- lm(accuracy_post        ~ accuracy_pre        + treatment, data = dat)
ancova_warm <- lm(warmth_outgroup_post ~ warmth_outgroup_pre + treatment, data = dat)
cooks_thresh <- 4 / nrow(dat)

dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    cooks_acc  = cooks.distance(ancova_acc),
    cooks_warm = cooks.distance(ancova_warm),
    flag_cooks = cooks_acc > cooks_thresh | cooks_warm > cooks_thresh
  )

# ── Mahalanobis distance on (acc_change, warm_change) ────────────────────────
# Chi-square threshold: p < .025, df = 2
mah_thresh <- qchisq(0.975, df = 2)
dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    mah_d    = mahalanobis(cbind(acc_change, warm_change),
                           colMeans(cbind(acc_change, warm_change), na.rm = TRUE),
                           cov(cbind(acc_change, warm_change), use = "complete.obs")),
    flag_mah = mah_d > mah_thresh
  )

ids_cooks <- dat$ResponseId[dat$flag_cooks]
ids_mah   <- dat$ResponseId[dat$flag_mah]
n_cooks   <- length(ids_cooks)
n_mah     <- length(ids_mah)
n_overlap <- sum(ids_mah %in% ids_cooks)   # Mah is a strict subset of Cook's D

# Use Mahalanobis for removal (more conservative; Cook's D subset already included)
outlier_ids <- ids_mah
n_before  <- nrow(dat)
dat       <- dat %>% filter(!ResponseId %in% outlier_ids)
n_removed <- n_before - nrow(dat)

# ── Regression-to-mean evidence (on cleaned data) ────────────────────────────
rtm_cors <- dat %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(r_pre_change = cor(accuracy_pre, acc_change, use = "complete.obs"),
            .groups = "drop")
rtm_cors_text <- rtm_cors %>%
  mutate(s = paste0(treatment, ": *r* = ", numformat(r_pre_change))) %>%
  pull(s) %>% paste(collapse = ", ")
Code 
dat_all <- bind_rows(
  dat,
  bind_rows(
    filter(read_parquet(paste0(base_path, if (full_data) "analysis_data.parquet" else "analysis_data_Feb17.parquet")),
           TRUE) %>%
      mutate(
        acc_change  = accuracy_post - accuracy_pre,
        warm_change = warmth_outgroup_post - warmth_outgroup_pre,
        treatment   = factor(if (pool_controls)
                               if_else(treatment == "Contact", "Contact", "Control")
                             else treatment,
                             levels = if (pool_controls) c("Contact","Control")
                                      else c("Contact","Chat","Game"))
      )
  ) %>% filter(ResponseId %in% c(ids_cooks, ids_mah))
) %>% distinct(ResponseId, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Rebuild Cook's D on full pre-removal data for plotting
dat_pre <- read_parquet(paste0(base_path, if (full_data) "analysis_data.parquet" else "analysis_data_Feb17.parquet")) %>%
  mutate(
    acc_change  = accuracy_post - accuracy_pre,
    warm_change = warmth_outgroup_post - warmth_outgroup_pre,
    treatment   = factor(if (pool_controls)
                           if_else(treatment == "Contact", "Contact", "Control")
                         else treatment,
                         levels = if (pool_controls) c("Contact","Control")
                                  else c("Contact","Chat","Game"))
  )

anc_acc_pre  <- lm(accuracy_post        ~ accuracy_pre        + treatment, data = dat_pre)
anc_warm_pre <- lm(warmth_outgroup_post ~ warmth_outgroup_pre + treatment, data = dat_pre)
mah_pre <- mahalanobis(
  cbind(dat_pre$acc_change, dat_pre$warm_change),
  colMeans(cbind(dat_pre$acc_change, dat_pre$warm_change), na.rm = TRUE),
  cov(cbind(dat_pre$acc_change, dat_pre$warm_change), use = "complete.obs")
)

plot_dat <- dat_pre %>%
  mutate(
    cooks_acc  = cooks.distance(anc_acc_pre),
    cooks_warm = cooks.distance(anc_warm_pre),
    mah_d      = mah_pre,
    flag_cooks = cooks_acc > cooks_thresh | cooks_warm > cooks_thresh,
    flag_mah   = mah_d > mah_thresh,
    idx        = row_number()
  )

# Cook's D panels
p_cook_acc <- ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = idx, y = cooks_acc, color = flag_cooks)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = cooks_thresh, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "gray60", "TRUE" = "#D73027"), guide = "none") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Cook's D", title = "Cook's D — Accuracy ANCOVA") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

p_cook_warm <- ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = idx, y = cooks_warm, color = flag_cooks)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = cooks_thresh, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40") +
  geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "gray60", "TRUE" = "#D73027"), guide = "none") +
  labs(x = "Participant index", y = "Cook's D", title = "Cook's D — Warmth ANCOVA") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

# Mahalanobis scatter
p_mah <- ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = acc_change, y = warm_change)) +
  stat_ellipse(level = 0.975, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(aes(color = flag_mah), size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = filter(plot_dat, flag_cooks & !flag_mah),
             shape = 1, size = 4, color = "#FC8D59", stroke = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "gray60", "TRUE" = "#D73027"),
                     labels = c("Retained", "Mahalanobis outlier"),
                     name = NULL) +
  annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = Inf, hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1.5, size = 3,
           label = "Open circles = Cook's D only (not Mah.)") +
  labs(x = "Accuracy change (post − pre)", y = "Warmth change (post − pre)",
       title = "Mahalanobis distance in change-score space") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

(p_cook_acc / p_cook_warm) | p_mah

Outlier methods compared. Cook’s D (threshold 4/n = 0.0100) flagged 56 participants across the two ANCOVA models. Mahalanobis distance on the joint (accuracy change, warmth change) space (χ² threshold p < .025, df = 2) flagged 20, all of whom were also flagged by Cook’s D. The remaining 36 Cook’s D flags were influential in one ANCOVA but not extreme in the bivariate change space. We use the Mahalanobis criterion for removal as it directly captures participants with unusual combinations of change scores, is scale-invariant, and results in a more conservative exclusion (N removed = 20). All analyses below use the cleaned sample (N = 381).

Baseline and change. Within each condition, participants who started with the lowest accuracy tended to show the largest improvements (Contact: r = -0.72, Control: r = -0.61). This is mechanically expected in any pre-post design and applies similarly across conditions, so it does not confound the condition comparison. The DiD contrast and ANCOVA below account for baseline by design.

Code 
desc_vars <- list(
  "Accuracy (pre)"        = "accuracy_pre",
  "Accuracy (post)"       = "accuracy_post",
  "Warmth (pre)"          = "warmth_outgroup_pre",
  "Warmth (post)"         = "warmth_outgroup_post",
  "Confidence (pre)"      = "confidence_outgroup_pre",
  "Confidence (post)"     = "confidence_outgroup_post",
  "Donation (cents)"      = "donation",
  "Age"                   = "age",
  "Political extremism"   = "political_extremism"
)

fmt_msd <- function(x) {
  m <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  s <- sd(x,   na.rm = TRUE)
  paste0(numformat(m), " (", numformat(s), ")")
}

conds <- levels(dat$treatment)

desc_tbl <- map_dfr(names(desc_vars), function(label) {
  col <- desc_vars[[label]]
  row <- tibble(Variable = label, Overall = fmt_msd(dat[[col]]))
  for (cond in conds) {
    row[[cond]] <- fmt_msd(dat[[col]][dat$treatment == cond])
  }
  row
})

desc_tbl %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Descriptive Statistics by Condition",
             subtitle = "M (SD); after Cook's D outlier removal") %>%
  tab_footnote("Accuracy = negative absolute error (higher = more accurate). Warmth on 0–100 scale. Confidence on 1–5 scale.",
               locations = cells_title())
Descriptive statistics (M ± SD) for all key variables overall and by condition, after outlier removal.

Accuracy (pre) -1.12 (0.83) -1.13 (0.83) -1.10 (0.84)
Accuracy (post) -0.77 (0.64) -0.76 (0.60) -0.78 (0.68)
Warmth (pre) 33.04 (25.99) 35.86 (26.20) 29.99 (25.49)
Warmth (post) 34.83 (27.10) 38.21 (27.42) 31.17 (26.35)
Confidence (pre) 3.12 (1.02) 3.08 (1.05) 3.16 (0.98)

Donation (cents) 20.15 (21.50) 20.13 (21.40) 20.17 (21.68)

Code 
ggplot(dat, aes(x = accuracy_pre, y = acc_change, color = treatment)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, alpha = 0.15) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors, name = "Condition") +
  facet_wrap(~ treatment) +
  labs(
    x = "Baseline Accuracy (pre)",
    y = "Accuracy Change (post − pre)",
    title = "Regression to the Mean: Accuracy Change vs. Baseline by Condition"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
Code 
ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "rtm_check.png"), width = 9, height = 4, dpi = 300)

Pre-Post Changes Overall

Before examining condition differences, we verify that the pre-post design shows evidence of change across the full sample.

Code 
# Accuracy
acc_overall <- t.test(dat$accuracy_post, dat$accuracy_pre, paired = TRUE)
acc_d_overall <- mean(dat$accuracy_post - dat$accuracy_pre) / sd(dat$accuracy_post - dat$accuracy_pre)

# Warmth
warm_overall <- t.test(dat$warmth_outgroup_post, dat$warmth_outgroup_pre, paired = TRUE)
warm_d_overall <- mean(dat$warmth_outgroup_post - dat$warmth_outgroup_pre) / sd(dat$warmth_outgroup_post - dat$warmth_outgroup_pre)

# Confidence
conf_overall <- t.test(dat$confidence_outgroup_post, dat$confidence_outgroup_pre, paired = TRUE)
conf_d_overall <- mean(dat$confidence_outgroup_post - dat$confidence_outgroup_pre) / sd(dat$confidence_outgroup_post - dat$confidence_outgroup_pre)

Across the full sample, accuracy improved (t(380) = 9.60, p < .001, d = 0.49), outgroup warmth increased (t(380) = 4.28, p < .001, d = 0.22), and confidence changed (t(380) = 4.94, p < .001, d = 0.25).

Condition Differences (Main Analysis)

The primary question is whether the Contact condition shows greater pre-post improvement than the two control conditions (Chat, Game) pooled together. We use a mixed-effects model with a time × treatment interaction, and test the key contrast: Contact Δ vs. average of Chat Δ and Game Δ.

Accuracy

Code 
accuracy_long <- dat %>%
  select(ResponseId, treatment, learner_party, accuracy_pre, accuracy_post) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(accuracy_pre, accuracy_post),
               names_to = "time", names_pattern = "accuracy_(pre|post)",
               values_to = "accuracy") %>%
  mutate(time = factor(time, levels = c("pre", "post")))

# Model: time × treatment (reference = Contact)
m_acc <- lmer(accuracy ~ time * treatment + (1 | ResponseId), data = accuracy_long)

# Pre-post differences within each condition
emm_acc_by_cond <- emmeans(m_acc, ~ time | treatment)
acc_contrasts <- contrast(emm_acc_by_cond, "revpairwise", by = "treatment") %>%
  as_tibble()  # positive = improvement (post - pre)

# Full grid for DiD contrasts — adaptive to pool_controls toggle
# Grid order (3-cond): pre×Contact, post×Contact, pre×Chat, post×Chat, pre×Game, post×Game
# Grid order (pooled): pre×Contact, post×Contact, pre×Control, post×Control
emm_acc_full <- emmeans(m_acc, ~ time * treatment)
pull_did <- function(tbl, nm, col) tbl %>% filter(contrast == nm) %>% pull({{ col }})

if (pool_controls) {
  acc_did <- contrast(emm_acc_full, list(
    `Contact vs. Control` = c(1, -1, -1, 1)   # Control Δ - Contact Δ ... sign: positive = Control > Contact
  ))
  # flip sign so positive = Contact improved more
  acc_did_tbl <- acc_did %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(estimate = -estimate)
  acc_main_b  <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", estimate)
  acc_main_se <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", SE)
  acc_main_p  <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", p.value)
  acc_chat_vs_contact_b <- NA_real_; acc_chat_vs_contact_p <- NA_real_
  acc_game_vs_contact_b <- NA_real_; acc_game_vs_contact_p <- NA_real_
  acc_chat_vs_game_b    <- NA_real_; acc_chat_vs_game_p    <- NA_real_
} else {
  acc_did <- contrast(emm_acc_full, list(
    `Contact vs. pooled controls` = c(-1,  1,  0.5, -0.5,  0.5, -0.5),
    `Chat vs. Contact`            = c( 1, -1, -1,    1,    0,    0  ),
    `Game vs. Contact`            = c( 1, -1,  0,    0,   -1,    1  ),
    `Chat vs. Game`               = c( 0,  0, -1,    1,    1,   -1  )
  ))
  acc_did_tbl           <- acc_did %>% as_tibble()
  acc_main_b            <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", estimate)
  acc_main_se           <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", SE)
  acc_main_p            <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", p.value)
  acc_chat_vs_contact_b <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Contact", estimate)
  acc_chat_vs_contact_p <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Contact", p.value)
  acc_game_vs_contact_b <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Game vs. Contact", estimate)
  acc_game_vs_contact_p <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Game vs. Contact", p.value)
  acc_chat_vs_game_b    <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Game",    estimate)
  acc_chat_vs_game_p    <- pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Game",    p.value)
}

The time × treatment interaction tests whether conditions differed in accuracy improvement.

Code 
acc_contrasts %>%
  select(Condition = treatment,
         `Pre→Post Improvement` = estimate,
         SE, df, `t-ratio` = t.ratio, `p-value` = p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    `Pre→Post Improvement` = numformat(`Pre→Post Improvement`),
    SE = numformat(SE),
    df = numformat(df, 0),
    `t-ratio` = numformat(`t-ratio`),
    `p-value` = map_chr(`p-value`, pformat)
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Accuracy: Pre-Post Improvement by Condition") %>%
  tab_footnote("Positive = improvement. Estimated from mixed-effects model (Contact as reference).",
               locations = cells_title())
Accuracy: Pre-Post Improvement by Condition1
Condition Pre→Post Improvement SE df t-ratio p-value
Contact 0.37 0.05 379 7.36 < .001
Control 0.33 0.05 379 6.20 < .001
1 Positive = improvement. Estimated from mixed-effects model (Contact as reference).

Primary test: Contact vs. Control: b = 0.05, SE = 0.07, p = 0.528.

Model choice: lmer vs. ANCOVA

Code 
# Pre-post correlation by condition (drives the key tradeoff between lmer and ANCOVA)
prepost_cors <- dat %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    r_prepost = cor(accuracy_pre, accuracy_post, use = "complete.obs"),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ANCOVA: regress post on pre + treatment
ancova_acc <- lm(accuracy_post ~ accuracy_pre + treatment, data = dat)
ancova_pre_coef <- coef(ancova_acc)["accuracy_pre"]
ancova_pre_se   <- summary(ancova_acc)$coefficients["accuracy_pre", "Std. Error"]
ancova_pre_p    <- summary(ancova_acc)$coefficients["accuracy_pre", "Pr(>|t|)"]

# Test homogeneity of regression slopes (pre × condition interaction)
ancova_hom   <- lm(accuracy_post ~ accuracy_pre * treatment, data = dat)
ancova_hom_p <- anova(ancova_acc, ancova_hom)$`Pr(>F)`[2]

if (!pool_controls) {
  ancova_game_b <- coef(ancova_acc)["treatmentGame"]
  ancova_chat_b <- coef(ancova_acc)["treatmentChat"]
  ancova_game_p <- summary(ancova_acc)$coefficients["treatmentGame", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  ancova_chat_p <- summary(ancova_acc)$coefficients["treatmentChat", "Pr(>|t|)"]
} else {
  ancova_game_b <- NA_real_; ancova_chat_b <- NA_real_
  ancova_game_p <- NA_real_; ancova_chat_p <- NA_real_
}

For a randomized pre-post design, both lmer(accuracy ~ time * treatment + (1|id)) and lm(accuracy_post ~ accuracy_pre + treatment) (ANCOVA) are valid, but they make different assumptions. ANCOVA is generally preferred in randomized experiments [@vickers2001analysing]. It regresses the post-score on the pre-score as a covariate, removing pre-score variance from the residuals and increasing statistical power. In our data, the pre-score coefficient is b = 0.43 (SE = 0.03, p < .001): substantially less than 1.0, which means there is regression to the mean and ANCOVA extracts more power than a change-score approach.

lmer (lmer(y ~ time * treatment + (1|id))) models both time points jointly with a random intercept per participant. The random intercept implicitly assumes the pre-score coefficient equals 1, equivalent to an ANCOVA with b = 1 for pre. When the true pre-post correlation is well below 1 — here r = 0.49 (Contact), r = 0.63 (Control) — the lmer model leaves avoidable residual variance on the table.

Homogeneity of slopes: ANCOVA requires the pre → post regression slope to be the same in all conditions. The pre × treatment interaction test gives p = 0.016. This assumption is borderline violated; lmer is more robust in this case.

Both models agree: conditions do not differ significantly in accuracy improvement. For the pre-registered full study, we will use ANCOVA as the primary specification and lmer as a robustness check.

Code 
# Build a side-by-side comparison table: lmer DiD vs ANCOVA
ancova_se_mat <- summary(ancova_acc)$coefficients

if (pool_controls) {
  # Only one contrast available: Contact vs. Control
  ancova_ctrl_b  <- coef(ancova_acc)["treatmentControl"]
  ancova_ctrl_se <- ancova_se_mat["treatmentControl", "Std. Error"]
  ancova_ctrl_p  <- ancova_se_mat["treatmentControl", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  model_compare <- tribble(
    ~Contrast,              ~`lmer b`, ~`lmer SE`, ~`lmer p`,
                             ~`ANCOVA b`, ~`ANCOVA SE`, ~`ANCOVA p`,
    "Contact vs. Control",
      acc_main_b,
      pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", SE),
      acc_main_p,
      -ancova_ctrl_b,        # flip sign: positive = Contact improved more
      ancova_ctrl_se,
      ancova_ctrl_p
  )
} else {
  model_compare <- tribble(
    ~Contrast,                    ~`lmer b`, ~`lmer SE`, ~`lmer p`,
                                   ~`ANCOVA b`, ~`ANCOVA SE`, ~`ANCOVA p`,
    "Contact vs. pooled controls",
      acc_main_b,
      pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", SE),
      acc_main_p,
      -(0.5 * ancova_chat_b + 0.5 * ancova_game_b),
      NA_real_, NA_real_,
    "Chat vs. Contact",
      acc_chat_vs_contact_b,
      pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Contact", SE),
      acc_chat_vs_contact_p,
      ancova_chat_b,
      ancova_se_mat["treatmentChat", "Std. Error"],
      ancova_chat_p,
    "Game vs. Contact",
      acc_game_vs_contact_b,
      pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Game vs. Contact", SE),
      acc_game_vs_contact_p,
      ancova_game_b,
      ancova_se_mat["treatmentGame", "Std. Error"],
      ancova_game_p,
    "Chat vs. Game",
      acc_chat_vs_game_b,
      pull_did(acc_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Game", SE),
      acc_chat_vs_game_p,
      ancova_chat_b - ancova_game_b,
      NA_real_, NA_real_
  )
}

model_compare %>%
  mutate(across(c(`lmer b`, `lmer SE`, `ANCOVA b`, `ANCOVA SE`),
                ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "—", numformat(.x))),
         `lmer p`   = map_chr(`lmer p`,   ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "—", pformat(.x))),
         `ANCOVA p` = map_chr(`ANCOVA p`, ~ ifelse(is.na(.x), "—", pformat(.x)))) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "lmer vs. ANCOVA: Accuracy Condition Comparisons") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "lmer (DiD contrast)", columns = c(`lmer b`, `lmer SE`, `lmer p`)) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "ANCOVA (lm post ~ pre + condition)", columns = c(`ANCOVA b`, `ANCOVA SE`, `ANCOVA p`)) %>%
  tab_footnote("lmer estimates from emmeans DiD contrasts on the full time × treatment grid. ANCOVA estimates are regression coefficients (contact = reference). Contact vs. pooled ANCOVA SE omitted (non-standard linear combination).",
               locations = cells_title())
lmer vs. ANCOVA: Accuracy Condition Comparisons1
Contrast
lmer (DiD contrast)
ANCOVA (lm post ~ pre + condition)
lmer b lmer SE lmer p ANCOVA b ANCOVA SE ANCOVA p
Contact vs. Control 0.05 0.07 = 0.528 0.03 0.05 = 0.607
1 lmer estimates from emmeans DiD contrasts on the full time × treatment grid. ANCOVA estimates are regression coefficients (contact = reference). Contact vs. pooled ANCOVA SE omitted (non-standard linear combination).
Code 
emm_acc_tbl <- as_tibble(emm_acc_by_cond) %>%
  mutate(time = factor(time, levels = c("pre", "post")))

p_acc <- ggplot(emm_acc_tbl,
                aes(x = time, y = emmean, group = treatment, color = treatment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.4) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = emmean - SE, ymax = emmean + SE), size = 0.6, linewidth = 0.8) +
  # Significance stars per condition (spaced by row index)
  geom_text(data = acc_contrasts %>%
              mutate(
                x = 1.5,
                y = max(emm_acc_tbl$emmean) + 0.5,
                label = p_to_stars(p.value),
                y_offset = (row_number() - 1) * -0.5
              ),
            aes(x = x, y = y + y_offset, label = label, color = treatment),
            size = 4, fontface = "bold", show.legend = FALSE, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors, name = "Condition") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Pre", "Post"), expand = expansion(add = 0.3)) +
  labs(y = "Accuracy (neg. abs. error)", x = NULL, title = "Accuracy by Condition") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_acc
Code 
ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "accuracy_by_condition.png"), p_acc, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

Warmth

Code 
warmth_long <- dat %>%
  select(ResponseId, treatment, learner_party, warmth_outgroup_pre, warmth_outgroup_post) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(warmth_outgroup_pre, warmth_outgroup_post),
               names_to = "time", names_pattern = "warmth_outgroup_(pre|post)",
               values_to = "warmth") %>%
  mutate(time = factor(time, levels = c("pre", "post")))

m_warm <- lmer(warmth ~ time * treatment + (1 | ResponseId), data = warmth_long)

emm_warm_by_cond <- emmeans(m_warm, ~ time | treatment)
warm_contrasts <- contrast(emm_warm_by_cond, "revpairwise", by = "treatment") %>%
  as_tibble()

emm_warm_full <- emmeans(m_warm, ~ time * treatment)

if (pool_controls) {
  warm_did <- contrast(emm_warm_full, list(
    `Contact vs. Control` = c(1, -1, -1, 1)
  ))
  warm_did_tbl <- warm_did %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(estimate = -estimate)
  warm_main_b  <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", estimate)
  warm_main_se <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", SE)
  warm_main_p  <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", p.value)
  warm_chat_vs_contact_b <- NA_real_; warm_chat_vs_contact_p <- NA_real_
  warm_game_vs_contact_b <- NA_real_; warm_game_vs_contact_p <- NA_real_
  warm_chat_vs_game_b    <- NA_real_; warm_chat_vs_game_p    <- NA_real_
} else {
  warm_did <- contrast(emm_warm_full, list(
    `Contact vs. pooled controls` = c(-1,  1,  0.5, -0.5,  0.5, -0.5),
    `Chat vs. Contact`            = c( 1, -1, -1,    1,    0,    0  ),
    `Game vs. Contact`            = c( 1, -1,  0,    0,   -1,    1  ),
    `Chat vs. Game`               = c( 0,  0, -1,    1,    1,   -1  )
  ))
  warm_did_tbl           <- warm_did %>% as_tibble()
  warm_main_b            <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", estimate)
  warm_main_se           <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", SE)
  warm_main_p            <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", p.value)
  warm_chat_vs_contact_b <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Contact", estimate)
  warm_chat_vs_contact_p <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Contact", p.value)
  warm_game_vs_contact_b <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Game vs. Contact", estimate)
  warm_game_vs_contact_p <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Game vs. Contact", p.value)
  warm_chat_vs_game_b    <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Game",    estimate)
  warm_chat_vs_game_p    <- pull_did(warm_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Game",    p.value)
}
Code 
warm_contrasts %>%
  select(Condition = treatment,
         `Pre→Post Improvement` = estimate,
         SE, df, `t-ratio` = t.ratio, `p-value` = p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    `Pre→Post Improvement` = numformat(`Pre→Post Improvement`),
    SE = numformat(SE),
    df = numformat(df, 0),
    `t-ratio` = numformat(`t-ratio`),
    `p-value` = map_chr(`p-value`, pformat)
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Warmth: Pre-Post Improvement by Condition") %>%
  tab_footnote("Positive = increase in outgroup warmth. Estimated from mixed-effects model.",
               locations = cells_title())
Warmth: Pre-Post Improvement by Condition1
Condition Pre→Post Improvement SE df t-ratio p-value
Contact 2.35 0.58 379 4.07 < .001
Control 1.17 0.60 379 1.96 = 0.051
1 Positive = increase in outgroup warmth. Estimated from mixed-effects model.

Primary test: Contact vs. Control: b = 1.17, SE = 0.83, p = 0.160.

Confidence

Code 
confidence_long <- dat %>%
  select(ResponseId, treatment, learner_party, confidence_outgroup_pre, confidence_outgroup_post) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(confidence_outgroup_pre, confidence_outgroup_post),
               names_to = "time", names_pattern = "confidence_outgroup_(pre|post)",
               values_to = "confidence") %>%
  mutate(time = factor(time, levels = c("pre", "post")))

m_conf <- lmer(confidence ~ time * treatment + (1 | ResponseId), data = confidence_long)

emm_conf_by_cond <- emmeans(m_conf, ~ time | treatment)
conf_contrasts <- contrast(emm_conf_by_cond, "revpairwise", by = "treatment") %>%
  as_tibble()

emm_conf_full <- emmeans(m_conf, ~ time * treatment)

if (pool_controls) {
  conf_did <- contrast(emm_conf_full, list(
    `Contact vs. Control` = c(1, -1, -1, 1)
  ))
  conf_did_tbl <- conf_did %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(estimate = -estimate)
  conf_main_b  <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", estimate)
  conf_main_se <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", SE)
  conf_main_p  <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", p.value)
  conf_chat_vs_contact_b <- NA_real_; conf_chat_vs_contact_p <- NA_real_
  conf_game_vs_contact_b <- NA_real_; conf_game_vs_contact_p <- NA_real_
  conf_chat_vs_game_b    <- NA_real_; conf_chat_vs_game_p    <- NA_real_
} else {
  conf_did <- contrast(emm_conf_full, list(
    `Contact vs. pooled controls` = c(-1,  1,  0.5, -0.5,  0.5, -0.5),
    `Chat vs. Contact`            = c( 1, -1, -1,    1,    0,    0  ),
    `Game vs. Contact`            = c( 1, -1,  0,    0,   -1,    1  ),
    `Chat vs. Game`               = c( 0,  0, -1,    1,    1,   -1  )
  ))
  conf_did_tbl           <- conf_did %>% as_tibble()
  conf_main_b            <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", estimate)
  conf_main_se           <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", SE)
  conf_main_p            <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Contact vs. pooled controls", p.value)
  conf_chat_vs_contact_b <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Contact", estimate)
  conf_chat_vs_contact_p <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Contact", p.value)
  conf_game_vs_contact_b <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Game vs. Contact", estimate)
  conf_game_vs_contact_p <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Game vs. Contact", p.value)
  conf_chat_vs_game_b    <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Game",    estimate)
  conf_chat_vs_game_p    <- pull_did(conf_did_tbl, "Chat vs. Game",    p.value)
}
Code 
conf_contrasts %>%
  select(Condition = treatment,
         `Pre→Post Change` = estimate,
         SE, df, `t-ratio` = t.ratio, `p-value` = p.value) %>%
  mutate(
    `Pre→Post Change` = numformat(`Pre→Post Change`),
    SE = numformat(SE),
    df = numformat(df, 0),
    `t-ratio` = numformat(`t-ratio`),
    `p-value` = map_chr(`p-value`, pformat)
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Confidence: Pre-Post Change by Condition") %>%
  tab_footnote("Positive = increase in confidence. Estimated from mixed-effects model.",
               locations = cells_title())

Condition Pre→Post Change SE df t-ratio p-value
Contact 0.39 0.07 379 5.21 < .001
Control 0.14 0.08 379 1.76 = 0.079
1 Positive = increase in confidence. Estimated from mixed-effects model.

Primary test: Contact vs. Control: b = 0.25, SE = 0.11, p = 0.020.

Behavioral Outcomes by Condition

Dictator Game Donation

Code 
dat <- dat %>% mutate(donated_any = as.integer(donation > 0))

don_by_condition <- dat %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    N          = n(),
    M          = mean(donation, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD         = sd(donation, na.rm = TRUE),
    SE         = SD / sqrt(N),
    pct_donated = mean(donated_any) * 100,
    mean_pos   = mean(donation[donation > 0], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Continuous donation: overall ANOVA + Tukey pairwise
don_aov <- aov(donation ~ treatment, data = dat)
don_aov_p <- summary(don_aov)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
don_aov_f <- summary(don_aov)[[1]][["F value"]][1]
don_pairwise <- dat %>% tukey_hsd(donation ~ treatment)

extract_tukey <- function(tbl, g1, g2) {
  tbl %>% filter(group1 == g1, group2 == g2)
}

# Stage 1 (any donation): overall ANOVA + Tukey pairwise
don_aov_bin <- aov(donated_any ~ treatment, data = dat)
don_aov_bin_p <- summary(don_aov_bin)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
don_aov_bin_f <- summary(don_aov_bin)[[1]][["F value"]][1]
don_pairwise_bin <- dat %>% tukey_hsd(donated_any ~ treatment)

# Stage 2 (amount among donors): ANOVA
dat_donors <- dat %>% filter(donated_any == 1)
don_aov_pos <- aov(donation ~ treatment, data = dat_donors)
don_aov_pos_p <- summary(don_aov_pos)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
don_aov_pos_f <- summary(don_aov_pos)[[1]][["F value"]][1]

# Condition-specific pairwise (only meaningful when 3 conditions)
if (!pool_controls) {
  don_cont_chat_game     <- extract_tukey(don_pairwise,     "Chat", "Game")
  don_bin_contact_chat_p <- extract_tukey(don_pairwise_bin, "Contact", "Chat")$p.adj
  don_bin_contact_game_p <- extract_tukey(don_pairwise_bin, "Contact", "Game")$p.adj
  don_bin_chat_game_p    <- extract_tukey(don_pairwise_bin, "Chat",    "Game")$p.adj
} else {
  don_cont_chat_game     <- tibble(p.adj = NA_real_)
  don_bin_contact_chat_p <- NA_real_
  don_bin_contact_game_p <- extract_tukey(don_pairwise_bin, "Contact", "Control")$p.adj
  don_bin_chat_game_p    <- NA_real_
}

Continuous donation: Conditions differed in donation amount (F = 0.00, p = 0.982).

Given that roughly half the sample donated nothing, we decompose into two stages. Stage 1 (any donation): conditions differed in whether participants donated at all (F = 0.03, p = 0.859); Contact vs. Control: p = 0.859. Stage 2 (amount among donors): among those who donated, conditions did not differ in how much they gave (F = 0.11, p = 0.746).

Code 
don_by_condition %>%
  mutate(across(c(M, SD, SE, mean_pos), numformat),
         pct_donated = numformat(pct_donated, 0)) %>%
  select(Condition = treatment, N,
         `% Donated` = pct_donated,
         `Mean (all)` = M, SD,
         `Mean (donors only)` = mean_pos) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Dictator Game: Donation by Condition") %>%
  tab_footnote("% Donated = proportion who gave any amount > 0. Mean (donors only) excludes zeros.",
               locations = cells_title())
Dictator Game: Donation by Condition1
Condition N % Donated Mean (all) SD Mean (donors only)
Contact 198 56 20.13 21.40 36.23
Control 183 55 20.17 21.68 36.92
1 % Donated = proportion who gave any amount > 0. Mean (donors only) excludes zeros.
Code 
bind_rows(
  don_pairwise %>% select(group1, group2, estimate, p.adj) %>% mutate(DV = "Continuous (0-50¢)"),
  don_pairwise_bin %>% select(group1, group2, estimate, p.adj) %>% mutate(DV = "Any donation (0/1)")
) %>%
  mutate(
    Comparison = paste0(group1, " vs. ", group2),
    estimate = numformat(estimate),
    p.adj = map_chr(p.adj, pformat)
  ) %>%
  select(DV, Comparison, `Difference` = estimate, `p (Tukey)` = p.adj) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(title = "Pairwise Tukey Comparisons: Donation") %>%
  tab_footnote("Tukey-corrected p-values.", locations = cells_title())
Pairwise Tukey Comparisons: Donation1
DV Comparison Difference p (Tukey)
Continuous (0-50¢) Contact vs. Control 0.05 = 0.982
Any donation (0/1) Contact vs. Control -0.01 = 0.859
1 Tukey-corrected p-values.
Code 
p_don_cont <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = treatment, y = donation, color = treatment)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.35, size = 2) +
  geom_pointrange(data = don_by_condition,
                  aes(x = treatment, y = M, ymin = M - SE, ymax = M + SE),
                  size = 0.8, linewidth = 1.2, inherit.aes = FALSE,
                  color = condition_colors[as.character(don_by_condition$treatment)]) +
  geom_text(data = don_by_condition,
            aes(x = treatment, y = M, label = numformat(M)),
            nudge_x = 0.3, size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE,
            color = condition_colors[as.character(don_by_condition$treatment)]) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
  labs(y = "Donation (cents)", x = NULL, title = "A. Continuous Amount") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# % donated panel with 95% CI
don_bin_ci <- don_by_condition %>%
  mutate(
    n_donated = round(pct_donated / 100 * N),
    ci_lo = map2_dbl(n_donated, N, ~ binom.test(.x, .y)$conf.int[1] * 100),
    ci_hi = map2_dbl(n_donated, N, ~ binom.test(.x, .y)$conf.int[2] * 100)
  )

p_don_bin <- ggplot(don_bin_ci, aes(x = treatment, y = pct_donated, color = treatment)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), size = 0.8, linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_hi + 4, label = paste0(round(pct_donated), "%")),
            size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  labs(y = "% Donating Any Amount", x = NULL, title = "B. Any Donation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_don <- p_don_cont + p_don_bin
p_don
Code 
ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "donation_by_condition.png"), p_don, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)

Talk Partner Preference

Code 
dat <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    chose_outgroup = case_when(
      participant_party == "Democrat"   & talk_partner == "I'd rather talk with a republican" ~ 1,
      participant_party == "Republican" & talk_partner == "I'd rather talk with a democrat"   ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    )
  )

talk_by_condition <- dat %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n_total      = n(),
    n_outgroup   = sum(chose_outgroup, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_outgroup = n_outgroup / n_total * 100,
    ci_lo = binom.test(n_outgroup, n_total)$conf.int[1] * 100,
    ci_hi = binom.test(n_outgroup, n_total)$conf.int[2] * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Overall chi-square
talk_tab <- table(dat$treatment, dat$chose_outgroup)
talk_chisq <- chisq.test(talk_tab)

# Pairwise Fisher's exact tests (only when 3 conditions exist)
if (!pool_controls) {
  fish_contact_chat <- fisher.test(talk_tab[c("Contact", "Chat"), ])
  fish_contact_game <- fisher.test(talk_tab[c("Contact", "Game"), ])
  fish_chat_game    <- fisher.test(talk_tab[c("Chat",    "Game"), ])
} else {
  fish_contact_chat <- list(p.value = NA_real_, estimate = NA_real_)
  fish_contact_game <- fisher.test(talk_tab[c("Contact", "Control"), ])
  fish_chat_game    <- list(p.value = NA_real_, estimate = NA_real_)
}
Code 
p_talk <- ggplot(talk_by_condition,
                 aes(x = treatment, y = pct_outgroup, color = treatment)) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi), size = 0.8, linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_hi + 5, label = paste0(round(pct_outgroup), "%")),
            size = 3.2, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 25)) +
  labs(y = "% Choosing Outgroup Partner", x = NULL,
       title = "Talk Partner Preference by Condition") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_talk
Code 
ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "talk_partner_by_condition.png"), p_talk, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

The overall chi-square test for condition differences in outgroup partner preference: χ² = 8.87, p = 0.003. Contact vs. Control: OR = 0.47, p = 0.002.

Combined Improvement Figure

Code 
# Abbreviated x-axis labels
x_labels <- if (pool_controls) {
  c("Contact" = "Co", "Control" = "Ctrl")
} else {
  c("Contact" = "Co", "Chat" = "Ch", "Game" = "Ga")
}

# Between-condition pairwise p-values from DiD contrasts
# Returns a tibble: group1, group2, p (numeric positions 1/2/3 on x-axis)
pairwise_did <- function(did_tbl) {
  if (pool_controls) {
    tibble(
      x1 = 1, x2 = 2,
      p  = pull_did(did_tbl, "Contact vs. Control", p.value)
    )
  } else {
    tibble(
      x1 = c(1, 1, 2),
      x2 = c(2, 3, 3),
      p  = c(
        pull_did(did_tbl, "Chat vs. Contact", p.value),
        pull_did(did_tbl, "Game vs. Contact", p.value),
        pull_did(did_tbl, "Chat vs. Game",    p.value)
      )
    )
  }
}

# Add significance brackets as annotation layers
# yrange: the observed (ymin, ymax) range of the pointranges
add_brackets <- function(pairs, ymax_data, y_scale = 1) {
  sig <- pairs %>% filter(p < .05) %>% mutate(stars = p_to_stars(p))
  if (nrow(sig) == 0) return(list())
  # Stack brackets above data
  bracket_gap  <- diff(range(ymax_data)) * 0.12 * y_scale
  bracket_step <- diff(range(ymax_data)) * 0.18 * y_scale
  top0 <- max(ymax_data) + bracket_gap
  layers <- list()
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(sig))) {
    row  <- sig[i, ]
    ytop <- top0 + (i - 1) * bracket_step
    # horizontal bar
    layers <- c(layers, list(
      annotate("segment", x = row$x1, xend = row$x2, y = ytop, yend = ytop,
               color = "gray30", linewidth = 0.5),
      # left tick
      annotate("segment", x = row$x1, xend = row$x1,
               y = ytop - bracket_gap * 0.4, yend = ytop,
               color = "gray30", linewidth = 0.5),
      # right tick
      annotate("segment", x = row$x2, xend = row$x2,
               y = ytop - bracket_gap * 0.4, yend = ytop,
               color = "gray30", linewidth = 0.5),
      # stars
      annotate("text", x = (row$x1 + row$x2) / 2, y = ytop + bracket_gap * 0.15,
               label = row$stars, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color = "gray20")
    ))
  }
  layers
}

# Base panel builder: within-condition dots + within-condition stars near point
make_panel <- function(contrasts_tbl, title, y_lab, pairs = NULL, y_scale = 1) {
  d <- contrasts_tbl %>%
    mutate(stars = p_to_stars(p.value), ymin = estimate - SE, ymax = estimate + SE)

  p <- d %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = treatment, y = estimate, color = treatment)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), size = 0.7, linewidth = 1.1) +
    geom_text(aes(y = ymax + diff(range(c(ymin, ymax))) * 0.10,
                  label = stars),
              size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = x_labels) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = y_lab, title = title) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

  if (!is.null(pairs)) {
    brks <- add_brackets(pairs, d$ymax, y_scale)
    for (lyr in brks) p <- p + lyr
  }
  p
}

acc_pairs  <- pairwise_did(acc_did_tbl)
warm_pairs <- pairwise_did(warm_did_tbl)
conf_pairs <- pairwise_did(conf_did_tbl)

pa <- make_panel(acc_contrasts,  "A. Accuracy",   "Improvement (neg. abs. error)", acc_pairs)
pb <- make_panel(warm_contrasts, "B. Warmth",     "Improvement (0-100 scale)",     warm_pairs)
pc <- make_panel(conf_contrasts, "C. Confidence", "Change (1-5 scale)",            conf_pairs)

# Donation panel: Tukey pairwise between conditions
don_pairs <- if (pool_controls) {
  tibble(x1 = 1, x2 = 2,
         p  = extract_tukey(don_pairwise, "Contact", "Control")$p.adj)
} else {
  tibble(
    x1 = c(1, 1, 2),
    x2 = c(2, 3, 3),
    p  = c(
      extract_tukey(don_pairwise, "Contact", "Chat")$p.adj,
      extract_tukey(don_pairwise, "Contact", "Game")$p.adj,
      extract_tukey(don_pairwise, "Chat",    "Game")$p.adj
    )
  )
}

don_d <- don_by_condition %>% mutate(ymin = M - SE, ymax = M + SE)
pd <- ggplot(don_d, aes(x = treatment, y = M, color = treatment)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), size = 0.7, linewidth = 1.1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = x_labels) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Donation (cents)", title = "D. Dictator Game") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
for (lyr in add_brackets(don_pairs, don_d$ymax)) pd <- pd + lyr

# Talk partner panel: Fisher exact pairwise
talk_pairs <- if (pool_controls) {
  tibble(x1 = 1, x2 = 2, p = fish_contact_game$p.value)
} else {
  tibble(
    x1 = c(1, 1, 2),
    x2 = c(2, 3, 3),
    p  = c(fish_contact_chat$p.value, fish_contact_game$p.value, fish_chat_game$p.value)
  )
}

talk_d <- talk_by_condition %>% mutate(ymin = ci_lo, ymax = ci_hi)
pe <- ggplot(talk_d, aes(x = treatment, y = pct_outgroup, color = treatment)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), size = 0.7, linewidth = 1.1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = x_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "% Outgroup", title = "E. Talk Partner") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
for (lyr in add_brackets(talk_pairs, talk_d$ymax, y_scale = 0.3)) pe <- pe + lyr

p_combined <- pa + pb + pc + pd + pe + plot_layout(ncol = 5)
ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "combined_by_condition.png"), p_combined, width = 14, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
p_combined
Code 
# ── Fit 3-way models for each DV ──────────────────────────────────────────────
m_acc_3way_cf  <- lmer(accuracy    ~ time * treatment * learner_party + (1 | ResponseId),
                        data = accuracy_long, REML = FALSE)
m_warm_3way_cf <- lmer(warmth      ~ time * treatment * learner_party + (1 | ResponseId),
                        data = warmth_long,   REML = FALSE)
m_conf_3way_cf <- lmer(confidence  ~ time * treatment * learner_party + (1 | ResponseId),
                        data = confidence_long, REML = FALSE)

# ── Extract DiD by party: post - pre emmean per condition × party ─────────────
party_did <- function(model, dv_label, y_label) {
  emm <- emmeans(model, ~ time | treatment * learner_party) %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    select(treatment, learner_party, time, emmean, SE)

  did <- emm %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = time, values_from = c(emmean, SE),
                id_cols = c(treatment, learner_party)) %>%
    mutate(
      estimate = emmean_post - emmean_pre,
      SE       = sqrt(SE_pre^2 + SE_post^2),
      ymin     = estimate - SE,
      ymax     = estimate + SE
    )

  dodge <- position_dodge(width = 0.4)

  ggplot(did, aes(x = treatment, y = estimate,
                  color = treatment, linetype = learner_party,
                  shape = learner_party, group = learner_party)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
                    size = 0.6, linewidth = 0.9,
                    position = dodge) +
    scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("D→R" = "solid", "R→D" = "dashed"),
                          name = "Learner party") +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("D→R" = 16, "R→D" = 17),
                       name = "Learner party") +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = x_labels) +
    guides(color = "none") +
    labs(x = NULL, y = y_label, title = dv_label) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.title = element_text(size = 10))
}

pa_p <- party_did(m_acc_3way_cf,  "A. Accuracy",   "Improvement (neg. abs. error)")
pb_p <- party_did(m_warm_3way_cf, "B. Warmth",     "Improvement (0-100 scale)")
pc_p <- party_did(m_conf_3way_cf, "C. Confidence", "Change (1-5 scale)")

# ── Donation by party: use raw means (no lmer for binary-ish DV) ────────────
don_party <- dat %>%
  group_by(treatment, learner_party) %>%
  summarise(M  = mean(donation, na.rm = TRUE),
            SE = sd(donation,   na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(ymin = M - SE, ymax = M + SE)

dodge <- position_dodge(width = 0.4)

pd_p <- ggplot(don_party, aes(x = treatment, y = M,
                               color = treatment, linetype = learner_party,
                               shape = learner_party, group = learner_party)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
                  size = 0.6, linewidth = 0.9,
                  position = dodge) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("D→R" = "solid", "R→D" = "dashed"),
                        name = "Learner party") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("D→R" = 16, "R→D" = 17),
                     name = "Learner party") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = x_labels) +
  guides(color = "none") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Donation (cents)", title = "D. Dictator Game") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10))

# ── Talk partner by party ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
talk_party <- dat %>%
  group_by(treatment, learner_party) %>%
  summarise(
    n_out = sum(chose_outgroup == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    n     = sum(!is.na(chose_outgroup)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    pct_outgroup = 100 * n_out / n,
    SE           = 100 * sqrt(pct_outgroup/100 * (1 - pct_outgroup/100) / n),
    ymin         = pmax(0,   pct_outgroup - SE),
    ymax         = pmin(100, pct_outgroup + SE)
  )

pe_p <- ggplot(talk_party, aes(x = treatment, y = pct_outgroup,
                                color = treatment, linetype = learner_party,
                                shape = learner_party, group = learner_party)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 50, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
                  size = 0.6, linewidth = 0.9,
                  position = dodge) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("D→R" = "solid", "R→D" = "dashed"),
                        name = "Learner party") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("D→R" = 16, "R→D" = 17),
                     name = "Learner party") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = x_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100)) +
  guides(color = "none") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "% Outgroup", title = "E. Talk Partner") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10))

p_combined_party <- pa_p + pb_p + pc_p + pd_p + pe_p +
  plot_layout(ncol = 5, guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "combined_by_condition_party.png"),
       p_combined_party, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 300)
p_combined_party

Moderating Role of Party

We also check whether the condition effects are moderated by learner party (D→R vs R→D).

Code 
# 3-way: time × treatment × party
accuracy_long_party <- accuracy_long

m_acc_3way <- lmer(accuracy ~ time * treatment * learner_party + (1 | ResponseId),
                   data = accuracy_long_party, REML = FALSE)
m_acc_2way <- lmer(accuracy ~ time * treatment + time * learner_party +
                     treatment * learner_party + (1 | ResponseId),
                   data = accuracy_long_party, REML = FALSE)

lrt_acc <- anova(m_acc_2way, m_acc_3way)
lrt_acc_p <- lrt_acc$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]

# Warmth 3-way
warmth_long_party <- warmth_long
m_warm_3way <- lmer(warmth ~ time * treatment * learner_party + (1 | ResponseId),
                    data = warmth_long_party, REML = FALSE)
m_warm_2way <- lmer(warmth ~ time * treatment + time * learner_party +
                      treatment * learner_party + (1 | ResponseId),
                    data = warmth_long_party, REML = FALSE)
lrt_warm <- anova(m_warm_2way, m_warm_3way)
lrt_warm_p <- lrt_warm$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]

A likelihood ratio test found that party did not significantly moderate the condition × time interaction for accuracy (p = 0.448) and did not significantly moderate it for warmth (p = 0.671).

Code 
emm_acc_party <- emmeans(m_acc_3way, ~ time | treatment * learner_party) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(time = factor(time, levels = c("pre", "post")))

ggplot(emm_acc_party,
       aes(x = time, y = emmean, group = interaction(treatment, learner_party),
           color = treatment, linetype = learner_party)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = emmean - SE, ymax = emmean + SE), size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ learner_party, labeller = labeller(learner_party = c("D→R" = "Democrats (D→R)", "R→D" = "Republicans (R→D)"))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors, name = "Condition") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("D→R" = "solid", "R→D" = "dashed"), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Pre", "Post"), expand = expansion(add = 0.3)) +
  labs(y = "Accuracy (neg. abs. error)", x = NULL,
       title = "Accuracy by Condition and Party") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "accuracy_condition_party.png"), width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)

  as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(time = factor(time, levels = c("pre", "post")))

ggplot(emm_warm_party,
       aes(x = time, y = emmean, group = interaction(treatment, learner_party),
           color = treatment, linetype = learner_party)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = emmean - SE, ymax = emmean + SE), size = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ learner_party, labeller = labeller(learner_party = c("D→R" = "Democrats (D→R)", "R→D" = "Republicans (R→D)"))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = condition_colors, name = "Condition") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("D→R" = "solid", "R→D" = "dashed"), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Pre", "Post"), expand = expansion(add = 0.3)) +
  labs(y = "Warmth toward outgroup (0–100)", x = NULL,

Code 
ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "warmth_condition_party.png"), width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)

Summary Table

Code 
# Compute condition-level summaries for key DVs
summary_rows <- bind_rows(
  # Accuracy
  dat %>% group_by(treatment) %>%
    summarise(DV = "Accuracy",
              Pre = mean(accuracy_pre, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              Post = mean(accuracy_post, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              Change = mean(accuracy_post - accuracy_pre, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              .groups="drop"),
  # Warmth
  dat %>% group_by(treatment) %>%
    summarise(DV = "Outgroup Warmth",
              Pre = mean(warmth_outgroup_pre, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              Post = mean(warmth_outgroup_post, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              Change = mean(warmth_outgroup_post - warmth_outgroup_pre, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              .groups="drop"),
  # Confidence
  dat %>% group_by(treatment) %>%
    summarise(DV = "Confidence",
              Pre = mean(confidence_outgroup_pre, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              Post = mean(confidence_outgroup_post, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              Change = mean(confidence_outgroup_post - confidence_outgroup_pre, na.rm=TRUE) %>% numformat(),
              .groups="drop")
) %>%
  select(DV, Condition = treatment, Pre, Post, Change)

summary_rows %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "DV") %>%
  tab_header(title = "Pre-Post Changes by Condition and DV") %>%
  tab_footnote("Change = Post − Pre. Positive = improvement/increase.",
               locations = cells_title())
Pre-post changes by condition across all dependent variables.
Pre-Post Changes by Condition and DV1
Condition Pre Post Change
Accuracy
Contact -1.13 -0.76 0.37
Control -1.10 -0.78 0.33
Outgroup Warmth
Contact 35.86 38.21 2.35
Control 29.99 31.17 1.17
Confidence
Contact 3.08 3.47 0.39
Control 3.16 3.30 0.14
1 Change = Post − Pre. Positive = improvement/increase.

Appendix: Simulation — lmer vs. ANCOVA Power and Bias

To understand which model better recovers a known treatment effect, we run a Monte Carlo simulation calibrated to this study’s design. We fix pre-scores to be equal across conditions (randomization holds), give the treatment condition a true post-score advantage, and ask: which model (a) recovers the true effect with less bias, and (b) detects it with higher power?

Code 
set.seed(2024)

# Parameters calibrated to observed data
n_trt_sim  <- n_contact   # ~126
n_ctrl_sim <- n_total - n_contact  # non-Contact participants
n_sims     <- 2

# From observed data
pre_mean <- mean(dat$accuracy_pre, na.rm = TRUE)
pre_sd   <- sd(dat$accuracy_pre,  na.rm = TRUE)
post_sd  <- sd(dat$accuracy_post, na.rm = TRUE)

# Use average pre-post correlation across conditions (pooled or per-condition)
if (!pool_controls) {
  # Exclude Chat outlier; average Contact and Game
  rho_sim <- mean(c(
    cor(dat$accuracy_pre[dat$treatment == "Contact"],
        dat$accuracy_post[dat$treatment == "Contact"], use = "complete.obs"),
    cor(dat$accuracy_pre[dat$treatment == "Game"],
        dat$accuracy_post[dat$treatment == "Game"], use = "complete.obs")
  ))
} else {
  rho_sim <- cor(dat$accuracy_pre, dat$accuracy_post, use = "complete.obs")
}

resid_sd <- post_sd * sqrt(1 - rho_sim^2)
b_pre_sim <- rho_sim * (post_sd / pre_sd)

# True effect sizes to sweep
true_effects <- c(0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.5)

sim_one <- function(true_delta) {
  # Pre: same in both groups
  pre_trt  <- rnorm(n_trt_sim,  pre_mean, pre_sd)
  pre_ctrl <- rnorm(n_ctrl_sim, pre_mean, pre_sd)

  # Post: true effect in treatment only
  post_trt  <- b_pre_sim * pre_trt  + true_delta + rnorm(n_trt_sim,  0, resid_sd)
  post_ctrl <- b_pre_sim * pre_ctrl +           0 + rnorm(n_ctrl_sim, 0, resid_sd)

  d <- data.frame(
    id        = seq_len(n_trt_sim + n_ctrl_sim),
    treatment = factor(c(rep("Treatment", n_trt_sim), rep("Control", n_ctrl_sim)),
                       levels = c("Control", "Treatment")),
    pre       = c(pre_trt,  pre_ctrl),
    post      = c(post_trt, post_ctrl)
  )

  # ANCOVA — unname() prevents name concatenation in replicate() matrix rownames
  anc     <- lm(post ~ pre + treatment, data = d)
  anc_est <- unname(coef(anc)["treatmentTreatment"])
  anc_p   <- unname(summary(anc)$coefficients["treatmentTreatment", "Pr(>|t|)"])

  # lmer
  d_long <- d %>%
    pivot_longer(c(pre, post), names_to = "time", values_to = "y") %>%
    mutate(time = factor(time, levels = c("pre", "post")))
  suppressMessages(
    m <- lmer(y ~ time * treatment + (1 | id), data = d_long)
  )
  emm      <- emmeans(m, ~ time * treatment)
  ctr      <- contrast(emm, list(did = c(-1, 1, 1, -1)))
  lmer_est <- unname(summary(ctr)$estimate)
  lmer_p   <- unname(summary(ctr)$p.value)

  c(anc_est = anc_est, anc_p = anc_p, lmer_est = lmer_est, lmer_p = lmer_p)
}

sim_results <- map_dfr(true_effects, function(delta) {
  # replicate returns a matrix: rows = named output elements, cols = simulations
  mat <- replicate(n_sims, sim_one(delta))  # dim: 4 x n_sims
  tibble(
    true_delta = delta,
    anc_bias   = mean(mat["anc_est", ])  - delta,
    anc_se     = sd(mat[  "anc_est", ]),
    anc_power  = mean(mat["anc_p",   ] < .05),
    lmer_bias  = mean(mat["lmer_est",]) - delta,
    lmer_se    = sd(mat[  "lmer_est",]),
    lmer_power = mean(mat["lmer_p",  ] < .05)
  )
})
Code 
sim_results %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(anc_bias, anc_se, lmer_bias, lmer_se), ~ numformat(.x, 3)),
    anc_power  = numformat(anc_power  * 100, 1),
    lmer_power = numformat(lmer_power * 100, 1),
    true_delta = numformat(true_delta, 2)
  ) %>%
  select(
    `True effect` = true_delta,
    `ANCOVA bias` = anc_bias, `ANCOVA SE` = anc_se, `ANCOVA power (%)` = anc_power,
    `lmer bias`   = lmer_bias, `lmer SE`   = lmer_se, `lmer power (%)`   = lmer_power
  ) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title    = "Simulation: ANCOVA vs. lmer — Bias, Efficiency, and Power",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "N = ", n_trt_sim, " (treatment) + ", n_ctrl_sim, " (control); ",
      "pre-post r ≈ ", numformat(rho_sim), "; ", n_sims, " simulations per effect size"
    )
  ) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "ANCOVA (lm post ~ pre + condition)",
              columns = c(`ANCOVA bias`, `ANCOVA SE`, `ANCOVA power (%)`)) %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "lmer (time × treatment + random intercept)",
              columns = c(`lmer bias`, `lmer SE`, `lmer power (%)`)) %>%
  tab_footnote(
    "Bias = mean(estimated) − true effect. SE = SD of estimates across simulations (lower = more efficient). Power = % of simulations with p < .05.",
    locations = cells_title()
  )
Simulation: ANCOVA vs. lmer — Bias, Efficiency, and Power1
N = 208 (treatment) + 193 (control); pre-post r ≈ 0.56; 2 simulations per effect size1
True effect
ANCOVA (lm post ~ pre + condition)
lmer (time × treatment + random intercept)
ANCOVA bias ANCOVA SE ANCOVA power (%) lmer bias lmer SE lmer power (%)
0.00 -0.007 0.004 0.0 -0.022 0.004 0.0
0.10 0.025 0.012 100.0 -0.211 0.082 50.0
0.20 -0.011 0.144 50.0 -0.409 0.114 50.0
0.30 0.006 0.080 100.0 -0.592 0.020 100.0
0.50 -0.040 0.003 100.0 -0.887 0.014 100.0
1 Bias = mean(estimated) − true effect. SE = SD of estimates across simulations (lower = more efficient). Power = % of simulations with p < .05.
Code 
sim_results %>%
  select(true_delta, anc_power, lmer_power) %>%
  pivot_longer(c(anc_power, lmer_power), names_to = "model", values_to = "power") %>%
  mutate(model = recode(model, "anc_power" = "ANCOVA", "lmer_power" = "lmer")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = true_delta, y = power * 100, color = model, group = model)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5,  linetype = "dashed", color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 80, linetype = "dotted", color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.01, y = 7,  label = "α = 5%",    size = 3, color = "gray50", hjust = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.01, y = 82, label = "80% power", size = 3, color = "gray50", hjust = 0) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("ANCOVA" = "#377EB8", "lmer" = "#E41A1C"), name = "Model") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20)) +
  labs(
    x = "True treatment effect (post-score units)",
    y = "Power (%)",
    title = "Statistical Power: ANCOVA vs. lmer"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
Code 
ggsave(paste0(fig_path, "simulation_power.png"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

Both estimators are unbiased: bias ≈ 0 regardless of the true effect. The key difference is efficiency: ANCOVA has a smaller SE and higher power because it explicitly removes pre-score variance from the residuals. The lmer random-intercept model implicitly assumes the pre → post slope equals 1; when the true slope is ~0.43 (as estimated from the data), lmer treats some explainable variance as noise. The power advantage of ANCOVA is consistent across effect sizes and is largest at intermediate effects (0.1–0.3), which is the realistic range for this paradigm.

Appendix: Outlier Robustness

How sensitive are the main DiD estimates to the choice of outlier exclusion strategy? We compare four samples: the full data, z-score exclusions (|within-condition z| > 2.5 on either change score), Cook’s D exclusions (> 4/n on either ANCOVA), and Mahalanobis exclusions (χ²(2) p < .025 on the joint change-score space). Only the 3-condition version is shown.

Code 
# ── Helper: fit all pairwise DiD contrasts for a given dataset + condition set ─
pairwise_did_contrasts <- function(d, conds) {
  d <- d %>% mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, levels = conds))
  n <- length(conds)

  fit_one <- function(dv_pre, dv_post, dv_label) {
    long <- d %>%
      select(ResponseId, treatment, all_of(c(dv_pre, dv_post))) %>%
      pivot_longer(c(all_of(dv_pre), all_of(dv_post)),
                   names_to = "time", values_to = "y") %>%
      mutate(time = factor(if_else(str_detect(time, "pre"), "pre", "post"),
                           levels = c("pre", "post")))
    m   <- lmer(y ~ time * treatment + (1 | ResponseId), data = long, REML = FALSE)
    emm <- emmeans(m, ~ time * treatment)
    pairs_idx <- combn(seq_along(conds), 2, simplify = FALSE)
    con_list  <- lapply(pairs_idx, function(idx) {
      v <- numeric(2 * n)
      i <- idx[1]; j <- idx[2]
      v[(2*i-1):(2*i)] <- c(-1,  1)
      v[(2*j-1):(2*j)] <- c( 1, -1)
      v
    })
    names(con_list) <- sapply(pairs_idx, function(idx)
      paste0(conds[idx[1]], " vs. ", conds[idx[2]]))
    contrast(emm, con_list) %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(dv = dv_label)
  }

  bind_rows(
    fit_one("accuracy_pre",        "accuracy_post",        "Accuracy"),
    fit_one("warmth_outgroup_pre",  "warmth_outgroup_post", "Warmth")
  )
}

# ── Build the four samples (always on raw full/Feb17 data, 3 conditions) ──────
dat_raw <- read_parquet(paste0(base_path,
  if (full_data) "analysis_data.parquet" else "analysis_data_Feb17.parquet")) %>%
  mutate(
    treatment   = factor(treatment, levels = c("Contact", "Chat", "Game")),
    acc_change  = accuracy_post - accuracy_pre,
    warm_change = warmth_outgroup_post - warmth_outgroup_pre
  )

# z-score flags
dat_raw <- dat_raw %>%
  group_by(treatment) %>%
  mutate(acc_z  = scale(acc_change)[, 1],
         warm_z = scale(warm_change)[, 1]) %>%
  ungroup()
ids_z_rob <- dat_raw$ResponseId[abs(dat_raw$acc_z) > 2.5 | abs(dat_raw$warm_z) > 2.5]

# Cook's D flags
anc_a <- lm(accuracy_post        ~ accuracy_pre        + treatment, data = dat_raw)
anc_w <- lm(warmth_outgroup_post ~ warmth_outgroup_pre + treatment, data = dat_raw)
ct    <- 4 / nrow(dat_raw)
ids_cd_rob <- dat_raw$ResponseId[cooks.distance(anc_a) > ct | cooks.distance(anc_w) > ct]

# Mahalanobis flags
mah_rob <- mahalanobis(
  cbind(dat_raw$acc_change, dat_raw$warm_change),
  colMeans(cbind(dat_raw$acc_change, dat_raw$warm_change), na.rm = TRUE),
  cov(cbind(dat_raw$acc_change, dat_raw$warm_change), use = "complete.obs")
)
ids_mah_rob <- dat_raw$ResponseId[mah_rob > qchisq(0.975, df = 2)]

conds_3 <- c("Contact", "Chat", "Game")

# ── Helper: Contact vs. pooled controls DiD ──────────────────────────────────
contact_vs_control_did <- function(d) {
  d <- d %>%
    mutate(trt2 = if_else(treatment == "Contact", "Contact", "Control"),
           trt2 = factor(trt2, levels = c("Contact", "Control")))

  fit_pooled <- function(dv_pre, dv_post, dv_label) {
    long <- d %>%
      select(ResponseId, trt2, all_of(c(dv_pre, dv_post))) %>%
      pivot_longer(c(all_of(dv_pre), all_of(dv_post)),
                   names_to = "time", values_to = "y") %>%
      mutate(time = factor(if_else(str_detect(time, "pre"), "pre", "post"),
                           levels = c("pre", "post")))
    m   <- lmer(y ~ time * trt2 + (1 | ResponseId), data = long, REML = FALSE)
    emm <- emmeans(m, ~ time * trt2)
    con <- list(`Contact vs. Control` = c(-1, 1, 1, -1))
    contrast(emm, con) %>% as_tibble() %>% mutate(dv = dv_label)
  }

  bind_rows(
    fit_pooled("accuracy_pre",       "accuracy_post",        "Accuracy"),
    fit_pooled("warmth_outgroup_pre", "warmth_outgroup_post", "Warmth")
  ) %>% mutate(contrast = "Contact vs. Control")
}

did_robust <- bind_rows(
  pairwise_did_contrasts(dat_raw,                                      conds_3) %>% mutate(sample = "Full sample"),
  pairwise_did_contrasts(filter(dat_raw, !ResponseId %in% ids_z_rob),  conds_3) %>% mutate(sample = "Z > 2.5 removed"),
  pairwise_did_contrasts(filter(dat_raw, !ResponseId %in% ids_cd_rob), conds_3) %>% mutate(sample = "Cook's D removed"),
  pairwise_did_contrasts(filter(dat_raw, !ResponseId %in% ids_mah_rob),conds_3) %>% mutate(sample = "Mahalanobis removed"),
  contact_vs_control_did(dat_raw)                                                %>% mutate(sample = "Full sample"),
  contact_vs_control_did(filter(dat_raw, !ResponseId %in% ids_z_rob))            %>% mutate(sample = "Z > 2.5 removed"),
  contact_vs_control_did(filter(dat_raw, !ResponseId %in% ids_cd_rob))           %>% mutate(sample = "Cook's D removed"),
  contact_vs_control_did(filter(dat_raw, !ResponseId %in% ids_mah_rob))          %>% mutate(sample = "Mahalanobis removed")
) %>%
  mutate(
    sample = factor(sample, levels = c("Full sample", "Z > 2.5 removed",
                                       "Cook's D removed", "Mahalanobis removed")),
    ymin   = estimate - SE,
    ymax   = estimate + SE,
    stars  = p_to_stars(p.value)
  )

# Per-panel star y position: add a small offset above the panel's own ymax
did_robust <- did_robust %>%
  group_by(dv, contrast) %>%
  mutate(star_y = ymax + (max(ymax) - min(ymin)) * 0.18) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(did_robust, aes(x = sample, y = estimate, color = sample)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), size = 0.55, linewidth = 0.85) +
  geom_text(aes(y = star_y, label = stars), size = 2.8, fontface = "bold") +
  facet_grid(dv ~ contrast, scales = "free") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2", name = NULL) +
  labs(x = NULL,
       y = "DiD estimate (positive = first-named condition improved more)",
       title = "Sensitivity of DiD estimates to outlier exclusion strategy") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.x  = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),
        strip.text.x = element_text(size = 9),
        strip.text.y = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"))
